evidence.scientific_clarity: Full 2-page prose Methods covers all STROBE items 4–12 with specific numbers (n=2,412 enrolled, 1,987 analytic, HR=1.5, 80% power, κ=0.78); text is clearly readable.
evidence.typography: LaTeX Computer Modern Type 1 fonts embedded (CMBX12, CMR10, CMMI10, CMTT10) — proper vector, no DejaVu Sans; one Type 3 font (F72, likely a rule/symbol) is present; fonttype=42 is a matplotlib setting and not applicable to pdflatex.
evidence.palette: Text-only document with no data visualisation; no colour palette to evaluate; no directional/categorical colour misuse possible.
evidence.layout_density: Dense 2-page prose with bold subsection leads, no gaping whitespace, no overlapping elements; paragraph structure follows Claim→Evidence→Interpretation template.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; STROBE coverage is explicit; however, main.tex (required output per brief) is absent from the working directory, and calling a text document a 'figure' may draw Reviewer 2 scrutiny.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdftotext extracts full prose confirming selectable vector text; PDF produced by pdfTeX-1.40.29 with embedded fonts; one Type 3 glyph (F72) slightly degrades full vector fidelity.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (73 KB) and figure.png (395 KB) both exist and are non-trivial; caption.tex and gen_figure.py present; main.tex is missing despite being a required deliverable in the brief.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states section type, venue (Cell Reports Medicine), sample size (n=2,412), and lists all STROBE items covered plus key statistical methods; provides a specific numerical claim.

overall_strengths: Exceptionally complete STROBE coverage: all eight Methods items (4–12) are addressed with study-specific detail including IPW, central re-read, E-value, MICE, Bonferroni, and R package versions.
Caption is informative and self-contained, naming the venue, sample size, and every STROBE item and method covered, allowing a reader to assess completeness without reading the full section.
overall_weaknesses: main.tex is absent from the working directory, violating the brief's explicit output requirement and preventing verification of the source prose independently of the compiled PDF.
One Type 3 font (F72) is embedded in the PDF alongside the otherwise clean Type 1 Computer Modern fonts; Type 3 fonts are bitmap-based and can cause rendering artefacts in print workflows.
